this game was pretty scary and fun at the same time, i will miss playing this game because how fun it was. i am going to be very sad that it has shut down, i have no idea why shut down but i'm guessing due to community hate and moderators this game was actually fun and i had no problem with it i never joined a public server mostly private with my friends but the game was overall good, follow my favorite map was the backrooms because that monster was so creepy he looks like a mimic and me and my friend ran so fast but always i was slow because i had short arms please bring back the game like at used to be, i'm gonna miss this game and i hope the fan game is gonna be like this. four stars because the monsters were only move if you went to them, the new lil moker right now it's okay but not is good is it better than the original but it's fine the monsters are scarier the jump scares are okay and all that stuff and at least they move on by themselves at least they don't go back to their previous spot running in place i would say the only game that me and my friend will only be playing right now is gorilla demise that's one of the fan games from the creator of monkrfunker  please bring back the game!

Munkr Funkr stands as a masterclass in the "experimental indie" genre because it captures the raw, unpolished soul of early VR—a period where creativity isn't stifled by corporate safety or predictable mechanics. It is absolute "peak" because it leans into its own absurdity, offering a physics-driven multiplayer experience that feels both nostalgic and refreshingly chaotic in 2026. The game’s brilliance lies in its rejection of modern gaming’s over-tutorialized hand-holding; instead, it throws you into a surreal world where the movement is the challenge and the community-driven interactions are the reward. Whether you are navigating its janky yet charming platforming sections or engaging in bizarre social tropes with other players, Munkr Funkr thrives on the kind of "lightning in a bottle" energy found in the Meta Store’s App Lab, proving that a game doesn't need a triple-A budget to be a definitive, high-tier VR experience. It is a fever dream made playable, and for those who value originality and pure, unfiltered fun over graphical fidelity, it remains an essential cornerstone of the JakobsGameStudio library.
